# gosling

gosling is an extensible, local-first AI agent forked from goose. It is independently maintained and is not affiliated with or endorsed by the goose project or AAIF.

gosling runs locally on your machine and connects to tools and services through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It is available as both a desktop application and a command-line interface (CLI).

## Key Features

- Local-first: runs on your machine with access to your development environment
- Extensible: adds capabilities through MCP extensions and servers
- Multi-provider: works with OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Ollama, and more
- Cross-platform: available on macOS, Linux, and Windows
- Open source: Apache 2.0 licensed, with upstream credit retained

## Core Concepts

- Extensions: plugins that give gosling new capabilities through MCP servers
- Sessions: conversation contexts that maintain state across interactions
- Providers: LLM backends that power gosling
- Goslinghints: project-specific instructions that customize gosling behavior

## Documentation

- Quickstart: /docs/quickstart
- Installation: /docs/getting-started/installation
- LLM Providers: /docs/getting-started/providers
- Using Extensions: /docs/getting-started/using-extensions
- CLI Commands: /docs/guides/gosling-cli-commands
- Tutorials: /docs/tutorials/
- MCP Servers/Extensions: /docs/mcp/

## Installation

### macOS/Linux/Windows (CLI)

```sh
curl -fsSL https://github.com/repo-makeover/gosling/releases/download/stable/download_cli.sh | bash
```

Desktop applications are available for macOS, Linux, and Windows at /docs/getting-started/installation.

## Common Use Cases

- Automating repetitive coding tasks
- Interacting with GitHub for PRs, issues, and code review
- Database queries and management
- File system operations
- Web scraping and API interactions
- CI/CD pipeline management
- Code generation and refactoring

## Built-in Extensions

- Developer: shell commands, file editing, and code analysis
- Computer Controller: system automation and browser interaction
- Memory: persistent storage across sessions
- Todo: task management within sessions
- Extension Manager: enable and disable extensions dynamically
- Chat Recall: search and reference past conversations

## Configuration

gosling configuration is stored in `~/.config/gosling/` with:

- `profiles.yaml`: provider and extension settings
- `config.yaml`: global preferences

Project-specific hints can be added via `.goslinghints` and/or `AGENTS.md` files.

## Links

- Website: https://gosling-docs.ai/
- GitHub: https://github.com/repo-makeover/gosling
- GitHub Discussions: https://github.com/repo-makeover/gosling/discussions
- Extensions Registry: https://gosling-docs.ai/extensions/
- Blog: https://gosling-docs.ai/blog/

## About

gosling is derived from goose, the open source AI agent from AAIF, and remains licensed under Apache 2.0. It is an independent fork.
